title: trifolium × adulterinum
intro: Trifolium × adulterinum, also known as the clover, is a hybrid plant that belongs to the pea family and is known for its trifoliate leaves. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Trifolium × adulterinum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a food source for livestock.
